behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff correctness (PR change-set selection -> file analysis input set). A unit test of only the selector can miss wiring bugs where analyzer still receives extra files. Rejected System because the behavior text only requires correct file selection, not a real external contract/readback (no explicit requirement to verify live GitHub response shapes or external side-effect visibility). Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline logic with a realistic PR diff fixture and repository snapshot, then assert analyzer invocation/file queue contains exactly changed files and excludes unchanged files.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Pass criteria is explicitly about what is in the posted PR comment, which is an external side effect that must be observed at the boundary (round-trip write/read). This triggers System TRIAD #3. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because grouping here is deterministic formatting/classification from linter severities, not model-dependent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the action against a real test PR, let it post the review comment via GitHub API, then read back the created comment and verify sections/grouping for error/warning/info match expected findings.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real CI runtime constraints (GitHub Actions timeout/environment), which unit/integration mocks cannot faithfully prove. The behavior explicitly anchors success to the Actions timeout boundary, requiring system-level execution. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the action in GitHub Actions on a PR fixture with ~200 changed files and assert end-to-end completion time is under 10 minutes from job start to final posted result.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly involves a crashing linter process (subprocess/OS behavior) plus externally visible posting of results. That requires real process-failure handling and boundary verification, satisfying System conditions. Rejected Integration because mocks can hide real crash/exit semantics. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decision quality is required.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a real linter crash on one malformed file during action execution, verify subsequent files are still processed, and confirm a PR comment is actually posted containing remaining-file findings plus crash reporting.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module merge/dedup logic (rule outputs -> aggregator -> rendered output). Unit tests for a dedup helper can miss integration bugs in key construction or merge ordering. Rejected System because behavior text says dedup in "the output" but does not explicitly require proving external posted-comment readback or other external contract checks. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML output dependency exists.
behaviors.test_description: Run multiple overlapping rule modules on a fixture file that triggers same-line duplicates, pass through full aggregation/render path, and assert final emitted findings contain one entry per unique rule+location policy.
